From capping overtime to encouraging flexible schedules to introducing 4-day work weeks, Japan is trying to foster a healthier work attitude. In a country notorious for overwork, will things change?

Daredevil motorcycle vigilantes who go on high-speed chases to catch criminals in Vietnam. While in Hong Kong, a group of citizens brave treacherous mountains as they search for a missing hiker.
For decades, China has been hit by natural disasters like typhoons, earthquakes, and floods. A group of ordinary citizens step into the disaster zones with the hope that more can be saved.
In Metro Manila, citizens form fire brigades to fight fires in vulnerable communities. They operate under uniquely challenging circumstances, becoming the unsung heroes of their communities.
From artisanal desserts to a city's costume change - watch Singapore flip from Christmas to Chinese New Year overnight, then feast on bak kwa and yu sheng for a prosperous start.

A worldwide scramble for rare earth metals broke out. Just why does China have such a grip on them? And why the worry? Well we need them not just for iPhones, but for everything electric in our lives.

Control over a group of coveted metals has made China king. But what happens when China defends its top spot? Presenter Kartik Kuna confronts the repercussions hidden in unlikely corners of the world.

Gallium is critical to green tech and China supplies 90% of it. So, when China began to restrict exports of gallium, it leads the world to eye other countries with this precious resource.
